recipes.title: Anchovy Straws
recipes.body:
## Notes

Keep the puff pastry cold while working with it. Freezing the assembled pastry for 15 minutes makes it easier to cut clean, even straws.

## Instructions

1. Brush one side of both sheets of puff pastry with beaten egg.
2. Arrange the anchovies from the entire tin in three evenly spaced ribbons across one sheet of puff pastry.
3. Sprinkle with Aleppo pepper and finely grated Parmesan.
4. Place the second sheet of puff pastry on top, egg-washed side facing down, to create a sandwich.
5. Transfer to the freezer for 15 minutes to firm up.
6. Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F) and line a baking tray with parchment paper.
7. Remove the pastry from the freezer and cut into 1 cm wide straws.
8. Place the straws on their sides on the lined baking tray.
9. Bake for 15 minutes, or until puffed, crisp, and golden brown.
10. Allow to cool slightly before serving.

recipes.title: Arayes – Meat Stuffed Pita
recipes.body:
## Notes

NA

## Instructions

1. Mix the feta cheese, herbs, and spices with the ground beef.
2. Cut the pita in half. Fill with meat and spread out evenly to desired thinness or thickness
3. Brush all sides of the filled pita with oil.
4. Preheat a pan or skillet over medium high heat.
5. Cook the arayes on each side (top, booth, and the open end) for 2-3 minutes until a dark golden brown color forms.
6. Slice in half and serve!

recipes.title: Cheesecake Stuffed Dates
recipes.body:
## Notes

Make sure the dates are completely dry before filling and coating them so the melted chocolate sticks properly.

Keep the cheesecake dates refrigerated until ready to serve.

## Instructions

1. Wash and thoroughly dry the dates.
2. Slice each date lengthwise and remove the pit, keeping the date intact.
3. Set aside 1 tablespoon of the crushed Lotus Biscoff biscuits for decorating.
4. In a small bowl, combine the mascarpone, remaining crushed biscuits, and vanilla extract.
5. Transfer the mascarpone mixture to a piping bag or use a small spoon to fill the center of each date.
6. Gently press the dates closed around the filling.
7. Melt the white chocolate until smooth.
8. Dip each filled date into the melted white chocolate until completely coated.
9. Place the chocolate-coated dates on a parchment-lined tray.
10. Sprinkle with the reserved crushed Lotus Biscoff biscuits before the chocolate sets.
11. Refrigerate until the chocolate is firm and keep chilled until ready to serve.

recipes.title: Crispy Pan-Fried Onigirazu
ingredients: 3 cups cooked short-grain white rice, seasoned with salt
recipes.body:
## Notes

Fair warning: regular tuna sandwiches are never going to hit the same after this.

## Instructions

1. Mix tuna with mayo, Sriracha, salt, and pepper. Set aside.
2. Fry the eggs and prep all fillings before building.
3. Dip 2 rice paper sheets in warm water, then overlap them on a cutting board. Layer 2 nori sheets on top.
4. Cut 2 slits to create 4 sections. Place rice in one section, tuna across from it, egg in the third, and avocado and cucumber in the fourth.
5. Fold rice over avocado, fold that stack onto the tuna, fold tuna over the egg, fold egg over to the rice, then fold rice over cucumber.
6. Wrap the whole square in 2 more wet rice paper sheets, press to seal, and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
7. Pan fry in neutral oil on medium-high heat, searing all sides until golden and crispy.
8. Slice in half.

recipes.title: Crunchy Mediterranean Roast Potatoes
ingredients: 4 potatoes, peeled and cut into cubes
1 teaspoon honey, plus more for serving
recipes.body:
## Notes

Parboiling the potatoes and shaking them after draining roughens the edges, helping them become extra crispy in the oven.

Preheating the vegetable oil in the baking tray helps the potatoes begin crisping as soon as they are added.

The lemon, oregano, and honey sauce can also be used as a dressing for salads, pasta, or other roasted vegetables.

## Instructions

1. Peel the potatoes and cut them into evenly sized cubes.
2. Place the potatoes in a pot, cover with water, add a sprinkle of salt, and bring to a boil. Cook for 15 minutes.
3. Preheat the oven to 200°C (400°F).
4. During the last 5 minutes of boiling the potatoes, pour enough vegetable oil into a deep baking tray to just cover the bottom. Place the tray in the oven for 5 minutes to heat the oil.
5. Meanwhile, combine the lemon zest, lemon juice, olive oil, oregano, chili flakes, salt, and honey in a small bowl. Mix well and set aside.
6. Drain the potatoes thoroughly and shake them vigorously in the pot or colander to roughen and fluff the edges.
7. Carefully add the potatoes to the baking tray with the hot oil and turn them to coat evenly.
8. Roast for 20 minutes, then remove the tray from the oven and turn the potatoes.
9. Return to the oven for another 20 minutes. Remove and turn the potatoes again.
10. Roast for another 20 minutes, then remove the tray and turn the potatoes once more.
11. Drizzle half of the lemon, oregano, and honey sauce over the potatoes and toss well to coat.
12. Return the potatoes to the oven for 5 minutes.
13. Allow the potatoes to cool slightly, then sprinkle generously with crumbled feta.
14. Drizzle with additional honey and serve with the remaining sauce as desired.

recipes.title: Fondant Potatoes
ingredients: 4 russet potatoes, peeled and cut into 1-inch rounds
recipes.body:
## Notes

Soaking the potatoes in cold water helps remove excess starch. Dry the potatoes thoroughly before searing to help them develop a deep golden-brown crust.

Use an oven-safe skillet so the potatoes can be transferred directly from the stovetop to the oven.

## Instructions

1. Peel the potatoes and trim the ends. Cut each potato in half crosswise to create 8 flat potato rounds.
2. Place the potatoes in a large bowl and cover with cold water. Soak for 20 minutes.
3. Arrange an oven rack in the middle of the oven and preheat to 400°F.
4. Drain the potatoes, rinse with cold water, and pat thoroughly dry with paper towels. Season with salt and black pepper.
5. Heat avocado oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
6. Add the potatoes, cut side down, and cook without moving for about 7 minutes, or until deeply golden brown.
7. Flip the potatoes and add the smashed garlic, butter, rosemary, and thyme.
8. Cook for 2-3 minutes, spooning the foaming butter over the potatoes, until the butter begins to brown.
9. Add the chicken stock so the potatoes are approximately halfway submerged. Bring the stock to a simmer.
10. Transfer the skillet to the oven and bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the potatoes are fork tender and lightly browned on all sides.
11. Spoon the garlic-herb butter and reduced cooking liquid over the potatoes and serve hot.

recipes.title: Laxpudding
recipes.body:
## Notes

NA

## Instructions

1. Peel the potatoes and boil in salted water until fork-tender. Let cool, then slice.
2. Thinly slice the onion and cook in the butter until soft, reserving the melted butter.
3. Whisk together cream, milk, and eggs, seasoning with salt and pepper.
4. Layer the sliced potatoes, smoked salmon, cooked onions, and fresh dill in a baking dish, pouring a bit of the cream mixture between each layer. Finish with a final layer of the cream mixture on top.
5. Bake at 175°C for about 30 minutes, until the top is golden and bubbly.
6. Drizzle with the reserved melted butter and garnish with a sprinkle of fresh dill.
I love serving it with a simple green salad tossed in lemon juice. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Meal Prep Soup Jars
ingredients: 2 cups cooked protein (chicken, pork, beef, or tofu), shredded or cubed
4 teaspoons Better than Bouillon chicken soup base (or 4 chicken bouillon cubes)
recipes.body:
## Notes

Store the prepared soup jars in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days.

Use 16-ounce heat-safe glass storage jars.

Quick-cook rice vermicelli or ramen noodles work best because they soften when hot water is added.

Allow the jars to come to room temperature before adding hot water to reduce the risk of the glass shattering. The water should be hot but not boiling.

## Instructions

1. To each 16-ounce glass jar, add 1 tablespoon soy sauce, 1 tablespoon miso paste, 1 teaspoon sriracha, 1 teaspoon sesame oil, 1 teaspoon Better than Bouillon chicken soup base (or 1 chicken bouillon cube), 1/4 teaspoon grated ginger, and 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder.
2. Mix the soup base ingredients with a small whisk or fork until well combined into a paste.
3. Divide the cooked protein evenly among the four jars.
4. Divide the carrots, bell peppers, edamame, and bok choy among the jars, adding the sturdier vegetables first.
5. Top each jar with green onions and cilantro.
6. Add one serving of quick-cook noodles to the top of each jar.
7. Seal the jars with tight-fitting lids and refrigerate for up to 3-4 days.
8. When ready to serve, remove a jar from the refrigerator and allow it to come to room temperature.
9. Slowly pour hot, but not boiling, water into the jar until all the ingredients are covered.
10. Close the lid and let the soup sit for 10 minutes. Use oven mitts or a towel when handling the hot jar.
11. Open the jar and stir thoroughly to dissolve the soup base and combine all the ingredients.
12. Enjoy directly from the jar or transfer to a bowl.

recipes.title: Lion's Mane Mushroom Steak Sandwich
recipes.body:
## Notes

The mushrooms can be marinated for anywhere from 30 minutes to 2 hours. A longer marinating time allows the mushrooms to absorb more flavor.

Pressing the lion's mane mushrooms while cooking removes moisture, creates a denser texture, and helps develop a golden-brown crust.

Use vegan ciabatta and crispy onions if you want to keep the entire sandwich vegan.

## Instructions

1. In a large tray, combine the beetroot juice, dark soy sauce, smoked paprika, maple syrup or agave, olive oil, brown miso paste, salt, and garlic powder. Mix well to make the marinade.
2. Heat a small amount of oil in a frying pan over medium heat.
3. Add the lion's mane mushrooms and cook for 5 minutes on each side.
4. As the mushrooms soften, gradually press them down with a spatula or another heavy pan to release moisture and flatten them into steak-like pieces.
5. Continue cooking and pressing until the mushrooms are golden brown on both sides.
6. Transfer the mushrooms to the marinade and turn to coat thoroughly. Marinate for 30 minutes to 2 hours.
7. Meanwhile, combine the wholegrain mustard, vegan mayonnaise, and chopped parsley in a small bowl.
8. Slice and toast the ciabatta bread if desired.
9. Heat a small amount of oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat.
10. Remove the mushrooms from the marinade and fry until deeply browned and charred around the edges.
11. Spread the mustard mayonnaise onto the ciabatta.
12. Add the rocket, charred lion's mane mushrooms, and crispy onions.
13. Close the sandwich and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Beef Pho Broth
ingredients: 5 pounds beef marrow bones and knuckle bones
1/4 cup fish sauce, plus more to taste
recipes.body:
## Notes

Keep the broth at a gentle simmer rather than a hard boil to produce a clear broth. Skim the surface regularly, especially during the first 1-2 hours of cooking.

The broth should taste slightly stronger and saltier than you want the finished bowl to taste because the rice noodles, meat, and garnishes will dilute the flavor.

## Instructions

1. Place the beef bones in a large stockpot and cover with cold water. Bring to a vigorous boil and boil for 3-5 minutes to release impurities.
2. Drain the bones and discard the water. Rinse the bones thoroughly with warm water and wash the stockpot to remove any residue.
3. Return the cleaned bones to the stockpot and add 5-6 quarts of fresh water. Bring to a boil, then immediately reduce the heat to maintain a gentle simmer.
4. Skim any foam, fat, and impurities that rise to the surface during the first hour of cooking.
5. Cut the onions and ginger in half. Char them under the broiler, over a gas flame, or in a dry cast-iron skillet until blackened in spots and fragrant.
6. Toast the star anise, cloves, cinnamon stick, and coriander seeds in a dry skillet over medium heat for 2-3 minutes until fragrant. Place the spices in a spice bag or piece of cheesecloth.
7. Add the charred onions, ginger, beef chuck or brisket, and spice bag to the stockpot.
8. Simmer gently for 1 1/2 hours, skimming the surface as needed.
9. Remove the beef chuck or brisket when tender. Let cool, thinly slice, and refrigerate for serving with the finished pho.
10. Continue simmering the bones, onions, ginger, and spices for another 2-4 hours. Do not allow the broth to boil vigorously.
11. Remove the bones, onions, ginger, and spice bag. Strain the broth through a fine-mesh strainer into a clean pot.
12. Add the rock sugar, salt, and fish sauce. Stir until the sugar dissolves, then taste and adjust the seasoning with additional fish sauce, salt, or sugar as needed.
13. If the broth has reduced significantly, add enough water to bring it back to approximately 5 quarts.
14. Serve hot over cooked rice noodles with thinly sliced beef and your preferred pho garnishes.

recipes.title: Red Lentil Protein Wraps
recipes.body:
## Notes

Each wrap contains approximately 9 g of protein and 112 calories, depending on the ingredients and brands used.

The lentils should soak for at least 1 hour before blending.

The batter will thicken as it sits. Blend it for a few seconds between cooking wraps if needed to restore a smooth consistency.

A high-quality non-stick pan may not require any oil.

## Instructions

1. Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under running water.
2. Place the lentils in a bowl with the warm water and soak for at least 1 hour.
3. Transfer the lentils and soaking water to a blender.
4. Add the spinach, nutritional yeast, and salt.
5. Blend for about 2 minutes, or until the batter is completely smooth and lump-free.
6. Heat a small amount of oil in a non-stick pan or skillet. If using a high-quality non-stick pan, the oil can be omitted.
7. Reduce the heat to medium-low and pour enough batter into the pan to create a thin layer.
8. Use the back of a spoon to spread the batter evenly across the surface of the pan if necessary.
9. Cook for 3-4 minutes, or until the edges begin to lift away from the pan.
10. Flip the wrap and cook for another 1-2 minutes.
11. Transfer the cooked wrap to a plate and repeat with the remaining batter.
12. If the batter thickens while sitting, blend it for a few seconds before cooking the next wrap.
13. Fill the wraps with your preferred protein, vegetables, sauces, and other toppings.

recipes.title: Tiramisu Balls
recipes.body:
## Notes

Chill the tiramisu mixture before coating if it is too soft to shape easily.

Keep the tiramisu balls refrigerated until ready to serve.

This recipe contains raw egg. Use a pasteurized egg if serving to pregnant people, young children, older adults, or anyone with a weakened immune system.

## Instructions

1. Separate the egg white from the yolk.
2. Beat the egg white until stiff peaks form.
3. In a separate bowl, whisk the egg yolk with the vanilla sugar until the mixture becomes pale and creamy.
4. Add the mascarpone to the egg yolk mixture and mix until smooth.
5. Gently fold the beaten egg white into the mascarpone mixture.
6. Add the coffee and gently mix until incorporated.
7. Finely crush or blend the ladyfinger biscuits.
8. Add the crushed ladyfingers to the mascarpone mixture and mix by hand until evenly combined.
9. Shape the mixture into 10 balls, or into balls of your preferred size.
10. Place the tiramisu balls in the refrigerator for 15 minutes to firm up.
11. Add the unsweetened cocoa powder to a small bowl.
12. Roll each chilled tiramisu ball in the cocoa powder until completely coated.
13. Refrigerate until ready to serve.
